incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — data extortion (ShinyHunters published stolen data)
incidents.vector: AWS API keys stolen via compromised Trivy vulnerability scanner (CVE-2026-33634) in TeamPCP supply chain campaign; initial access March 19, detected March 24.
incidents.impact: 340 GB of data exfiltrated from Europa web hosting platform including approximately 52,000 email-related files. 71 clients affected: 42 internal Commission departments plus 29 other EU entities.

incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — ransomware and data extortion (TeamPCP / CipherForce)
incidents.vector: Supply chain compromise via Trivy (CVE-2026-33634); CipherForce ransomware deployed in joint TeamPCP-Vect operation.
incidents.impact: 26,000 users' personal data and 23,169 athlete records exposed. Client table listing 161 organizations including ESPN, Nike, and NBA Asia leaked. 328 API key/secret pairs and 8 production database passwords exposed.

incidents.attacker: Iran — Ministry of Intelligence (MOIS)-linked hacktivist proxies
incidents.vector: Data-wiping attack by Handala group; exact initial access vector not disclosed. Group claimed retaliation for Feb. 28 missile strike on Iranian school.
incidents.impact: Offices in 79 countries reportedly disrupted; group claimed data wiped from 200,000+ systems. Over 5,000 workers sent home in Ireland. Order processing, manufacturing and shipping impacted for three weeks. Stryker now reports it is 'fully operational.'

incidents.vector: Security compromise via LiteLLM supply chain attack, part of the broader TeamPCP credential harvest campaign.
incidents.impact: Data breach confirmed; LAPSUS$ claimed hundreds of GB of data including Slack logs, ticketing records, and contractor biometric verification materials (passports, video interviews). Data listed for live auction. Class action investigation launched.

incidents.vector: Compromised lead maintainer's NPM account to publish malicious axios versions (1.14.1 and 0.30.4) with hidden RAT via fake 'plain-crypto-js' dependency. Attributed to UNC1069 by Google Threat Intelligence Group.
incidents.impact: Axios has 400M+ monthly NPM downloads; malicious versions were live for approximately 3 hours. Cross-platform RAT deployed to Windows, Linux, and Mac. Estimated 600,000 potential downloads during exposure window. Self-deleting anti-forensic cleanup.

incidents.vector: CVE-2025-53521 reclassified from DoS to RCE (CVSS 9.8) in F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager. Active exploitation in the wild; CISA added to KEV catalog.
incidents.impact: Unauthenticated remote code execution on affected BIG-IP APM deployments. Malware deployment observed. Scanning activity increasing as more threat actors map F5 infrastructure.

incidents.vector: Breach claimed by LAPSUS$; data initially offered for sale via Session encrypted messaging, then released for free after failed sale attempt.
incidents.impact: 3 GB archive released containing GitHub developer data, employee records from clinical research subsidiaries, and internal source code structures. Partially verified by Cybernews. AstraZeneca has not confirmed or denied the breach after 96+ hours.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized network access discovered March 28; exact vector not disclosed. SEC 8-K filing indicates likely ransomware based on recovery timeline.
incidents.impact: Some systems taken offline; business continuity plans activated to maintain orders and shipping. Full recovery expected to take several weeks with potential delays.

incidents.vector: Handala group targeted FBI Director's personal email account; FBI confirmed the targeting. Group claimed retaliation for domain seizures and $10M reward.
incidents.impact: Handala claims to have obtained personal and confidential emails, conversations, and documents. FBI states information is 'historical in nature' with no government information compromised. Email cache published by Distributed Denial of Secrets.

incidents.vector: CVE-2026-3055 (CVSS 9.3) in Citrix NetScaler ADC — unauthenticated memory leak similar to previous 'Citrix Bleed' bugs. Active exploitation reported by watchTowr; CISA ordered federal agencies to patch.
incidents.impact: Attackers can read sensitive memory from NetScaler deployments without authentication. Previously exploited against PA Attorney General's office and Netherlands' Public Prosecution Service.

incidents.victim: Software supply chain (Trivy, LiteLLM, Checkmarx, Telnyx, npm ecosystem)
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — data extortion and ransomware (TeamPCP)
incidents.vector: Multi-stage supply chain campaign beginning with compromise of Aqua Security's Trivy scanner via GitHub Actions misconfiguration. Spread to 5 ecosystems: GitHub Actions, PyPI, npm, Docker Hub/GHCR, and OpenVSX.
incidents.impact: Over 1,000 SaaS environments actively impacted per Mandiant. Estimated 500,000 machines affected and ~300 GB of stolen credentials circulating. Dual ransomware operations (CipherForce + Vect) now monetizing stolen access. CISA KEV deadline April 8.

incidents.vector: Exploited CVE-2026-3502 in TrueConf video conferencing software's update mechanism to distribute malicious payloads via legitimate update channel. Used Havoc penetration testing tool and ShadowPad malware.
incidents.impact: Dozens of government entities across an unnamed Southeast Asian country compromised via a single government-operated TrueConf server. Campaign focused on espionage; CISA ordered federal agencies to patch by April 16.

incidents.victim: City of Minot, North Dakota (water treatment plant)
incidents.vector: Ransomware deployed to water treatment plant server; ransom note left on screen. Exact initial vector not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Affected server unplugged; 16 hours of manual operations with on-location water gauge checks. Water safety and pressure maintained throughout. FBI investigating. No ransom paid.

incidents.attacker: Russia — GRU military intelligence and FSB intelligence service
incidents.vector: Russian hackers revisiting previously breached infrastructure to check if access persists and credentials remain valid. Increasingly using phone calls with Ukrainian mobile numbers and fluent Ukrainian to build trust before sending malware.
incidents.impact: CERT-UA warns that attackers are successfully regaining access to previously compromised systems where root causes were not fully remediated. Security and defense sector remains primary target.

incidents.vector: Russia-based actors targeting high-risk individuals' messaging app accounts through phishing, device linking, QR code attacks, and impersonation of known contacts.
incidents.impact: NCSC UK and international partners issued joint warning. Targets include government officials and individuals with access to sensitive information. Attackers attempt to add devices to accounts, join group chats undetected, or steal login codes.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP's CanisterWorm deploys a wiper payload that activates if the victim's timezone matches Iran or Farsi is the default language. Spreads through exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, and Redis servers.
incidents.impact: If the wiper detects Iran-matching settings and Kubernetes access, it destroys data on every node in the cluster. Otherwise wipes the local machine. Represents an unusual case of a financially motivated group deploying politically targeted destruction.

incidents.victim: DOJ Botnet Takedown (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad botnets)
incidents.vector: Four botnets compromised 3M+ IoT devices (routers, web cameras) to launch DDoS attacks, including against DOD infrastructure. Operators demanded extortion payments.
incidents.impact: DOJ, Canada, and Germany dismantled infrastructure. Aisuru issued 200,000+ attack commands; JackSkid 90,000+. Victims reported tens of thousands of dollars in losses. Law enforcement action designed to prevent future infections.

incidents.victim: Healthcare, government, hospitality, and education organizations (Germany, Canada, US, Australia)
incidents.vector: Phishing emails disguised as copyright infringement notices deliver PureLog Stealer via multistage fileless in-memory execution with AMSI bypass, anti-VM checks, and heavy obfuscation.
incidents.impact: Credential theft targeting Chrome browser credentials, crypto wallets, and system information. Campaign demonstrates selective targeting with locally-translated lures rather than mass distribution.
